content.claim: The Hungarian teaching workforce is ageing (50% of teachers are age 50+, only 7.2% under 30) and is experiencing documented attrition, particularly in schools serving ethnic-minority students, with a 2023 Status Law change permitting involuntary teacher transfers to address shortages.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Direct causal link between Status Law and rural-school staffing outcomes specifically was not found in sources; this is an inference
No data on net effect of rehiring retired teachers (2022 rule relaxation) on age distribution or turnover
Motivation and retention effects of involuntary transfer policy are not quantified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
